summary: Depth-guard lint covers key invariants but relies on substring checks and omits several required prompt/tool validations. Exit handling is implicit and lacks negative test cases.
covered: Worker frontmatter bans task and ask_user
Worker invariants: NEVER ask user questions, complete one task before next, DO NOT attempt to spawn sub-agents, You are a LEAF NODE
Worker launch permission bans: can_launch true variants
Commander metaswarm invariants: per_commander_full_swarm, swarm_scale, max_workers, 50x5x250 sizing, premium model policy incl gpt-5.5, child-agents.jsonl, launch_proof_required
Commander includes worker prompt fragments: DO NOT use the task tool, You are a LEAF NODE
gaps: Commander worker-prompt validation only checks 2 phrases; missing full 4-line depth-lock block (no checks for 'DO NOT attempt to spawn sub-agents, child agents, or any other agents.' and 'DO NOT delegate work. Complete your task YOURSELF using only your own tools.' and 'This instruction is non-negotiable.')
No exact/block/ordering enforcement for the required depth-lock prompt; substring checks allow scattered or paraphrased text
Worker banned-tool detection is whitespace-sensitive and frontmatter-only; no body-level scan and no commander prompt check for ask_user
Launch permission detection limited to three can_launch spellings; no validation commander explicitly sets can_launch true
Exit handling relies on implicit success and SystemExit string messages; no explicit exit-code assertions or negative test harness
No checks for additional depth-guard phrases like placeholder bans or 500-word limit (if required elsewhere)
exit_code_notes: Script uses set -euo pipefail and SystemExit for failures; implicit success via end of script
No explicit numeric exit codes or success assertions
No negative test cases that inject bad data to validate failures
recommendations: Add exact, contiguous block verification for the full worker depth-lock prompt in commander and worker contexts
Harden tool detection with regex/robust YAML parsing and include ask_user checks in commander worker prompt
Add explicit positive/negative tests or fixtures to validate failure paths and exit codes
